title: Aristocracy

The '''Aristocracy''' was the autocratic government of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Virgillia system/Legends|Virgillia system]]. From [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]] until [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], it was engaged in a [[Virgillian Civil War|civil war]] against the [[Virgillian Free Alignment/Legends|Virgillian Free Alignment]]. During that conflict, the Aristocracy controlled all [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] technology in the [[Star system/Legends|star system]], restricting the Free Alignment's access to [[Weapon/Legends|weaponry]]. The Aristocracy eventually lost the war, and the [[Virgillian Council]] was created to govern the system instead.

==Description==
The Aristocracy ruled the [[Virgillia system/Legends|Virgillia system]],<ref name="Bakura Sourcebook" /> which was located in the [[Koradin sector/Legends|Koradin sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Online Companion">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> The government was an autocracy made up of [[Virgillian]] [[Noble/Legends|nobility]]. As the Aristocracy was aligned with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], it had access to Imperial [[Capital ship/Legends|capital]] [[Warship/Legends|warships]], although once the Empire pulled out of the [[Star system/Legends|system]] after the intensification of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Virgillian government was left with [[Starship/Legends|ships]] that were outdated and unable to defeat the forces of the [[Virgillian Free Alignment/Legends|Virgillian Free Alignment]].<ref name="Bakura Sourcebook" />

==History==
[[File:Virgillia-TEA.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The Aristocracy governed the Outer Rim Territories' Virgillia system.]]
At some point by [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]], the Aristocracy gained power over the Virgillia system, and in that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the [[Virgillian Civil War]] broke out between it and the Virgillian Free Alignment.<ref name="HNN date 23" /> [[23 BBY/Legends|Two years]] later, a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] envoy consisting of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] [[Everen Ettene]] and [[Ludwin Katarkus]], along with their respective [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]], [[Halagad Ventor]] and [[Danyawarra]],<ref name="HNN">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|45|jediwatch/13228_1.html|Virgillian Jedi Envoy Declared Lost}}</ref> was sent to [[Virgillia 7]],<ref name="Skyewalkers">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref> a small [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] in the system,<ref name="Bakura Sourcebook" /> to [[Mission to Virgillia 7|negotiate peace]]. They were attacked upon arrival,<ref name="HNN" /> and Ettene<ref name="Skyewalkers" /> and Katarkus were [[Death/Legends|killed]].<ref name="CSWE Danyawarra">{{CSWECite|I|165|Danyawarra}}</ref>

In the wake of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Declaration of a New Order|fall]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> the Aristocracy aligned itself with the Galactic Empire,<ref name="Bakura Sourcebook" /> the Republic's successor.<ref name="Warfare" /> Ships of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], including the [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Stormwind]]'' under the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Jarroth]], patrolled the system, searching for Free Alignment activity. After the outbreak of the Galactic Civil War, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] began assisting the Free Alignment,<ref name="Bakura Sourcebook" /> and the Imperial Navy also began to search for any Alliance presence in the system.<ref name="CSWE Jarroth">{{CSWECite|II|141|Jarroth, Captain}}</ref>

During the Virgillian Civil War, the Aristocracy controlled all [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] technology in the Virgillia system, forcing the Free Alignment to rely only on primitive [[Weapon/Legends|weaponry]], such as [[Virgillian wheel lock|flintlocks]], and kept the Free Alignment confined to the system's colony worlds. As the Galactic Civil War intensified, the Empire retreated from the system, which greatly hindered the Aristocracy's ability to fight. After the Imperial withdrawal, the Free Alignment won enough battles that it was able to force the Aristocracy to agree to a truce shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="Bakura Sourcebook" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="Warfare" /> After the establishment of the truce, the [[Virgillian Council]] was created to govern the system.<ref name="Bakura Sourcebook" />

==Members==
The parents of [[Rannd Delckis]], who was educated at an [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]] and joined the Imperial Navy as an [[Ensign/Legends|ensign]], were high-ranking nobles who were members of the Aristocracy. Delckis eventually defected to the Free Alignment.<ref name="Bakura Sourcebook" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Aristocracy was first mentioned in ''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] guide published in [[1996]] by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The book was written by [[Kathy Tyers]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann]].<ref name="Bakura Sourcebook" />
